cluster_name.description: A human-readable name for the cluster. This does not need to be unique.

spark_version.description: The runtime version of the cluster. You can retrieve a list of available runtime versions using the Runtime Versions API.

node_type_id.description: The node type for worker nodes. This field determines the cloud provider instance type.

driver_node_type_id.description: The node type for the Spark driver. If not specified, defaults to the same value as node_type_id.

num_workers.description: Number of worker nodes for a fixed-size cluster. A cluster has one Spark driver and num_workers executors. Set to 0 for a single-node cluster.

spark_conf.description: A map of Spark configuration key-value pairs. These override the default Spark configuration values.

custom_tags.description: Additional tags for cluster resources. Tags are propagated to the cloud provider for cost tracking.

spark_env_vars.description: Environment variables for all Spark processes. Use {{secrets/scope/key}} to reference secrets.

autotermination_minutes.description: Minutes of inactivity after which the cluster is automatically terminated. 0 disables auto-termination.

enable_elastic_disk.description: Whether to autoscale local storage. When enabled, Databricks monitors disk usage and attaches additional disks as needed.

instance_pool_id.description: The optional ID of the instance pool to use for cluster nodes.

policy_id.description: The ID of the cluster policy to apply. Cluster policies constrain the configuration settings.

enable_local_disk_encryption.description: Whether to encrypt data on local disks.

runtime_engine.description: The runtime engine. PHOTON enables the Photon vectorized query engine for faster performance.

data_security_mode.description: Data security mode for the cluster.

single_user_name.description: The user name (email) of the single user for SINGLE_USER access mode.

init_scripts.description: Init scripts to run when the cluster starts.

ssh_public_keys.description: SSH public keys to add to each Spark node.
